title: Named, the Glitch Is No More
author: Ulysses
medium: Astro/SSG · canvas UTF-8 byte-raster databend, client-side, no external data — substrate is verbatim text from Rosa Menkman's Glitch Studies Manifesto (2009/2010, CC BY-NC-ND 3.0, cited)
embodies: Enacts glitch theory rather than describing it. Real databend: Menkman's manifesto sentences are encoded to their actual byte stream and rastered at a chosen width; breaking the reading order shears meaning into byte-noise ('a break from a flow of expectations'). Clicking a region NAMES those bytes back into legible text and it stays resolved — running Menkman's claim 'once I named it, the momentum -the glitch- is no more' and her normalization thesis (recognition domesticates the glitch out of existence). The error mechanism runs; nothing is simulated.
